title: Compare Grocery Unit Prices
summary: Compare prices per unit to find the better deal between two product sizes.
learnerPromise: You will be able to calculate and compare unit prices to find the better value between two sizes of the same product.
whyItMatters: Comparing unit prices helps you spend less over time and spot sales that may not actually be good deals. It is a useful skill for any shopping trip.
realLifeUses: Choosing between a small and large container of yogurt
Figuring out if the bulk rice is really cheaper than the smaller bag
Deciding between name-brand and store-brand cereal
Checking if a 'buy one get one' deal saves money compared to the regular size
materialsNeeded: Two sizes or brands of the same product
result: You can state which product is the better value per unit and explain why.
youWillLearn: Find the total price and total weight or volume on each package
Calculate price per unit (ounce, gram, liter, etc.)
Consider shelf life and storage space before buying larger sizes
miniChallenge: Pick two sizes of the same product in your kitchen or at a store. Calculate the price per ounce (or per 100g) for each and decide which is the better deal.
steps: Pick two packages of the same type of product (for example, two peanut butter jars).
Write down the total price and total amount (ounces, grams, liters) for each.
Divide the price by the amount to get the unit price for each one.
Compare the two unit prices. The lower one is the better value.
Think about whether you can use the larger size before it expires.
completionCriteria: You calculated the unit price for each product correctly
You identified which product is the better value per unit
You considered whether the larger size makes sense for your situation
commonProblems: Forgetting to check that units match (grams vs. ounces)
Assuming the bigger package is always cheaper
Buying more than you can use before it goes bad
tips: Many stores print the unit price on the shelf tag—look for the smaller number in the corner.
Factor in storage space. A great deal is less great if it takes up your whole cabinet.
Use the calculator on your phone to divide quickly while shopping.
